name: ON Power
verdictText: ON Power just signed with Monta (Dec 2025 tender) after migrating off Etrel. They are mid-migration — so a direct sales push now would land badly. Worth monitoring for the post-Monta evaluation window, especially given their stated ambitions in truck charging and deep ecosystem integration. The home + public + workplace bundle is a genuine long-term angle.

overview.category: Energy utility — EV charging is one division alongside energy retail
overview.primaryUseCase: Neighbourhood/destination charging; home subscription; workplace with employee reimbursements
overview.dcEstimate: 53 fast charging stations; a few 100–150kW en-route; some highway
overview.acEstimate: Majority of 200–250 public stations are 22kW AC; home chargers via Zaptec/Easee

network.totalStations: 200–250 public stations across Iceland; 53 fast charging locations
network.ultraFastDC: Some 100–150kW en-route; Autel ultra-fast partnership in 2025 (truck-focused)
network.ac: 22kW AC dominant — neighbourhood and destination focus; few highway stations
network.roamingNote: Roaming not prominently featured. Iceland is a closed market — most drivers charge at home or workplace. Highway fast charging is limited by geography.

partnerships.label: Etrel (Landis+Gyr)
partnerships.value: Previous CPMS — partnership started 2023; now being migrated away from

partnerships.label: Monta
partnerships.value: New CPMS — signed Dec 2025 after tender process

partnerships.label: Autel Europe (2025)
partnerships.value: Ultra-fast charger partnership; truck/freight charging ambition

app.currentApp: ON App (migrating — previously on Etrel app)
app.payment: App payments; subscription billing (home + workplace); one combined electricity + EV bill
app.subscriptions: Home charging subscription (2,900 ISK/month single home; 4,400 ISK/month apartment); business subscriptions available
app.support: Not specified — likely internal given utility scale
app.reviewNote: Previous app (Etrel): payment problems, crashes when paying unpaid invoices, unpredictable session stops. Migration to Monta app is in progress — timeline a few months.

techStack.value: Monta — signed Dec 2025 after competitive tender
Zaptec Pro, Zaptec Go, Easee Charge
Autel Europe (new — 2025); existing fast chargers brand TBC
EV charging billed with household electricity — single bill
Dynamic Load Management — core product feature and key selling point

siteHost.targetHosts: Businesses, stores, restaurants, housing associations, apartment blocks
siteHost.revenueModel: Subscription model — ON installs and maintains; customer pays monthly fee; station upgrades included
siteHost.valueProp: "Charger as a subscription" — installation, maintenance, upgrade included; cancel with 1 month notice; portable to new address

revenueStreams.label: Business subscriptions
revenueStreams.value: Monthly retainer; DC chargers available on subscription

revenueStreams.label: Combined energy billing
revenueStreams.value: EV charging bundled with household electricity bill — sticky retention mechanism

revenueStreams.label: Employee reimbursements
revenueStreams.value: Home charging reimbursement for employees — B2B fleet angle

revenueStreams.label: Housing association billing
revenueStreams.value: ON handles cost splitting between residents — value-add service

funding.label: Parent
funding.value: ON Power — Icelandic energy utility (Orka náttúrunnar)

funding.label: Expansion signal
funding.value: Truck charging investment (Autel partnership 2025) — 'first mover' ambition

policy.label: Market position
policy.value: Dominant energy utility in Iceland; main EV charging competitors are Ísorka and Charge365

policy.label: Truck ambition
policy.value: "Our goal is to lead the energy transition and build infrastructure that supports emerging technologies — even before they reach mainstream use in Iceland."

signals.text: Just signed Monta — Dec 2025. Mid-migration from Etrel to Monta. A direct approach now would land poorly. This is a monitoring situation — revisit once migration settles and early Monta friction surfaces.

Ecosystem integration is the core commercial strategy. Combined electricity + EV bill, portable subscriptions, housing association billing — every feature ties the customer deeper into ON. Whatever CPMS they use must support this tightly.

Truck charging is the next frontier. 2025 Autel partnership signals intent to be first in Iceland for truck/freight charging. This is a different CPMS requirement set — worth flagging as a future angle if Monta doesn't cover it well.
Home + public + workplace is already live. Full-stack CPO. Any platform competing with Monta needs to cover all three use cases plus employee reimbursements and combined billing.

App quality drove the Etrel departure. Payment problems and crashes were the stated reason for switching. Monta will be on the hook for the same expectations — if Monta underdelivers, there's an opening.

openQuestions: What specifically triggered the Monta tender — was it primarily app quality, or CPMS feature gaps?
What are the Monta contract terms — length, exit clauses?
Is truck charging being scoped as a separate system or expected to run on the same CPMS?
Does the combined electricity + EV billing require a custom Monta integration, or is it native?
Who runs the CPMS vendor relationship at ON — CTO, Head of EV, or product team?

hypotheses.label: Monta will create new friction — revisit in 12–18 months
hypotheses.text: ON moved because Etrel's app quality was poor. Monta is a strong product but ON's requirements — combined billing, housing association cost splits, subscription portability, truck charging — are complex. If Monta can't deliver all of it natively, dissatisfaction will surface.

hypotheses.label: Ecosystem lock-in is the product strategy — the CPMS must support it
hypotheses.text: Every ON feature is designed to keep customers in their ecosystem (portable subscriptions, bundled billing, upgrade guarantees). A CPMS that enables more of these hooks — not fewer — would be the right pitch.

hypotheses.label: Truck charging creates a new evaluation moment
hypotheses.text: Truck charging has materially different CPMS requirements (depot management, longer sessions, load management at scale). If Monta doesn't have strong truck support, ON will look elsewhere for that vertical — creating a wedge.

uncertainties: Monta contract is brand new — realistic migration window is probably 2+ years away.
Iceland is a small market — absolute station numbers and revenue are modest.
Truck charging ambition may not materialize quickly enough to create near-term opportunity.

contacts.name: Head of EV Charging / CTO
contacts.title: Identify via LinkedIn (on.is)
contacts.note: ON is a utility — the EV charging decision-maker is likely a divisional head, not C-suite CEO.

synthesis.contactNote: Worth tracking the Monta rollout via app store reviews and LinkedIn activity. Re-engage when migration dust settles (estimate: late 2026).
